pattern: Precompute the running leader after each timestamped vote once, then every query is a binary search for the latest vote at or before the queried time, turning repeated lookups into logarithmic work.
hints: The leader can only change at the moments a vote is cast, so there is a finite list of (time, leader) snapshots to consult.
Build, in one pass, the leader after each vote, breaking ties toward the candidate whose most recent vote is newest.
Store the sorted vote times and the matching leader after each one; a query at time t becomes a binary search for the rightmost time not exceeding t, returning that snapshot's leader.

edge_inputs.description: leader changes and ties resolve to the most recent vote
query exactly on a vote time counts that vote
a tie where the trailing candidate's newer vote takes the lead
query before but reaching the first vote time
later votes flip and unflip the leader across queries

stress.description: many votes with strictly increasing times over few candidates, then many queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    nvotes = max(1, size // 2)
    cand = rng.randint(1, 5)
    persons = [rng.randrange(cand) for _ in range(nvotes)]
    t = 0
    times = []
    for _ in range(nvotes):
        t += rng.randint(1, 5)
        times.append(t)
    ops = ['TopVotedCandidate']
    args = [[persons, times]]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        ops.append('q')
        args.append([rng.randint(times[0], t)])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: strictly increasing times and few candidates force frequent lead changes and ties
